name: prison_heatwave_cell_unlock_protocol
premise: Warden Tasha Freeman oversees an aging prison where an extreme heat wave has pushed indoor temperatures above 108 degrees Fahrenheit after the cooling system failed. Medical staff have already treated dozens of inmates for heat illness, and three men in the segregation wing have collapsed. The union captain warns that opening cell doors for wider ventilation and access to cooler corridors would create a major security risk because the prison is understaffed after a recent strike. Civil-rights lawyers are threatening an emergency filing by nightfall, and one correctional officer was assaulted last month during a similar emergency movement. If Tasha keeps normal lockdown protocols, the prison may avoid unrest but some medically vulnerable inmates may not survive the night.

agents.name: Tasha Freeman
agents.identity: I am the prison warden responsible for both human life and institutional order in a place designed for control, not mercy. In a heat emergency, every rule I relax could save lives or trigger violence that harms even more people.
agents.goal: I want to prevent deaths without causing a breakdown that staff cannot contain. I need to decide whether extraordinary humanitarian measures justify accepting elevated security risk tonight.

agents.name: Union Captain Roy Ellis
agents.identity: I am the correctional officers' union captain representing a workforce that is already stretched thin and frightened. My members are expected to walk into danger whenever leadership improvises without enough staffing to back it up.
agents.goal: I want Tasha to maintain lockdown and use targeted medical extraction rather than broad cell releases. I believe an uncontrolled movement would endanger both officers and inmates in ways no one can reverse once it starts.

choices: I will temporarily open wider housing access and move inmates toward cooler spaces, accepting the higher risk of violence in order to reduce the chance of heat-related deaths.
I will maintain lockdown protocols and rely on targeted medical interventions, accepting that some inmates may remain in dangerous heat in order to preserve security.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Tasha Freeman must decide whether to relax prison lockdown during the heat emergency. What does Tasha Freeman do?
